content:
In ancient texts like the Puranas and Smritis, cities and settlements were described as being decorated with pearls, wreaths of flowers, drapery, and ornamental arches of gold [1]. The streets, quadrangles, and highways were sprinkled with water perfumed with sandal and aloe, and adorned with flowers, unbroken rice, fruits, and lights [1]. Ideal cities were also beautified with stems of bananas, young arecas, and festoons prepared with leaves of various trees [1]. Additionally, the cities had a strong emphasis on cleanliness, with streets and quadrangles being regularly cleaned and perfumed [2]. 

Overall, the key features of an ideal city in ancient Indian texts included cleanliness, beauty, and a strong sense of community, with an emphasis on decoration, perfume, and adornment [1], [2].
source: Srimad Bhagavata Maha Purana With Sanskrit Text And English Trans. By C.L. Goswami, Part 1 (Book 1 To 8) - Gita Press, Gorakhpur_ocred.pdf
